name: command-center
tagline: ClawHub (79★): Mission control dashboard for OpenClaw - real-time session monitoring, LLM usage tracking, cost intelligence, and system vit
verdict: The entire flow assumes the happy path - no guidance for missing node/npx, a port already in use, a failed install, or bad auth. It also pulls an unpinned @latest package from clawhub, a niche registry rather than a well-known one - a mild supply-chain concern - and gives no scope boundaries.

highlights.summary: No guidance anywhere for missing node/npx, port-in-use, failed npx install, or auth misconfiguration — the whole flow assumes the happy path.
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill language anywhere in the file; the skill covers many broad features (sessions, cost, cron, topics) so misfire on adjacent monit
Unpinned @latest from clawhub, a niche/less-established package source rather than a well-known dependency — no registry override or typosquat evident, so warning rather than fail
